Re
Farn Polrucel PnacrrcEs C ounrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0986
January 11,2006
Kristine Loomis
21-200 Toltec Way
Desert Hot Springs, CA 92241
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-217
Dear Ms. Loomis:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the economic
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the.Act,)J
QUESTION
What are your reporting obligations in connection with a special needs trust
established for you based on your disabilities?
CONCLUSION
You must disclose reportable interests held by the trust, including sources of
income received by the trust of $500 or more during the reporting period, or gifts to the
trust of $50 or more. In addition, if the fair market value olu.r"tr of the truslequals or
exceeds $2,000, you must also report the trust on your statement of economic interests.
FACTS
You were appointed for a volunteer position on the In Home Support Services
Advisory Committee for Riverside County on October 4h, and,this position requires full
disclosure.
You are named recipient for the Kristine Loomis Special Needs Trust (the
"trust"), a trust that presently holds almost no monetary assets (a couple hundred dollars)
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections
1 8 I 09- I 8997, of the California Code of Regulations.
ffii:.n


File No. A-05-217
Page No. 2
and no real property or other investments. Over the past yea4 the trust had income
somewhere in the range of several hundred dollars (without contacting the accountant,
you can say with certainty it was less than $2,000). Income to the trust during the last
year would have been from a potential employer who donated funds that couli be used
on your behalf for the purchase of computer equipment. The trust was set up because
you are severely physically disabled and need to remain eligible for government services
(Medical and In Home Support Services) but need u -eurs by which others may help
you, or you may inherit, without disquali$ring you from those seryices. This type of t ort
(in your understanding) means that any asset it holds is not legally yours but may be used
by the trustee for certain specific needs on your behalf. The trustle is your husband.
However it is also your understanding that any asset held by the trust is not legally your
husband's either.
AIIALYSIS
Your question concerns your reporting obligations in connection with a special
needs trust. This type of trust is not treated as a business entity under the Act bui may
constitute an "investment" for purposes of the Act. The term "investment,, inbludes a pro
rata share of investments of anybusiness entity, mutual fund, or trust in which an
individual or immediate family owns, directly, indirectly or benefici ally, a 10-percent
interest or greater. (Section 52034.) Assets shall be deemed an inveshnent oniy if the
fair market value equals or exceeds $2,000.
Under the provisions of sect ion87206,you are required to report investments, but
only if your pro rata share of the fair market value equals or exceeds $Z,OOO. (Section
87206(d).) In addition, you are required to report income and gifts. (Section ilzOl.T
The term "income" includes the pro rata share of any incorne (including gifts) to a trust in
which an individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly or benefici atty, itO-percent
interest or greater. (Section 82030.)
Regulation l8%a@)describes the circumstances in which property, income, gifts,
and investments of a trust may be considered among a public official's economic
interests. Regulation 18234 states:
"(a) An official has an economic interest in the pro rutashare of
the interests in real property, sources of income, and investments of a trust
in which the official has a direct, indirect, or beneficial interest of
10 percent or greater."
Regulation ISTa@) further states:
"(c) For purposes of determining whether an official has an
economic interest in interests in real property, sources of income, and


File No. A-05-217
Page No. 3
investments of a trust, the official has a direct, indirect, or beneficial
interest in a trust if the official is:
,,l1...fl
"(2) A beneficiary and:
'(A) presently receives income; or
"(B) Has an irrevocable fufure right to receive income orprincipal.
For purposes of this subsection, an individual has an irrevocable iuture
right to receive income or principal if the trust is irrevocable, unless one of
the following applies:
"(i) Powers exist to consume, invade, or appoint the principal for
the benefit of beneficiaries other than the official and ruct po*er.'-" rot
limited by an ascertainable standard relating to the health, e'ducation,
support, or maintenance of the beneficiaries; or
"(ii) Under the terms of the trust, someone other than the official
can designate the persons who shall possess or enjoy the trust property or
income."
You indicate that you are the beneficiary of l\o% of the trust and that you
presently receive payments from and use assetsin the trust. Therefore, u"Jrr tlr"
rationale of regulation 18234, we would consider you to have a beneficial interest in the
trust of a l0-percent interest or greater for reporting putposes. consequently, you must
disclose any reportable interests held/receiv.h uy t[e trust.2 Fo. examfil, vi,l -"u
disclose sources of income to the trust of $500 or more on schedul" C of ior- 700 or
sources of gifts of $50 or more on schedule E.3
As to the trult, t_ty question posed in your letter is whether you would be required
to disclose the trust itself on schedule A-2. Based on the definition of ,oinvestment,,, we
conclude that you would have to report the trust itself if the fair market value of assets
held by the trust reaches the $2,000 threshold during the reporting period. (See generally,
Witehurst Advice Letter, No. I-95-360.) 
I 
e r -----' \-vY
2 We note that nothing in your letter indicates that this is a business trust. (Section g2005.)
Therefore, the $10,000 reporting threshold for sources of income to business entitie; is noiappiicaute in
this case., (Section 87207(b).)
'Note that the threshold for the gift limit and disqualification from a decision is $360. The gift
limit and the threshold for disqualifi catioi ari ialusted biennially to reflect changes in the Consumer price
Index' The limit will next be adjusted in2006. (s.^".!g189503(b; regulation n6+o.z.y ro uaajtion,
effective Januaty 7,2006, the Form 700 for 2oo5/2oo6tras ueen ievirZd to -orr" gift reporting to
schedule D.


File No. A-05-217
Page No. 4
If you have arry other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
John W. Wallace
, Assistant General Counsel
..,r Legal Division

QUESTION: What are your reporting obligations in connection with a special needs trust
established for you based on your disabilities?

CONCLUSION: You must disclose reportable interests held by the trust, including sources of
income received by the trust of $500 or more during the reporting period, or gifts to the
trust of $50 or more. In addition, if the fair market value olu.r"tr of the truslequals or
exceeds $2,000, you must also report the trust on your statement of economic interests.
